candidate_findings.claim: In Scene A, Rawn arrives after dark and does not light the second lamp, suggesting dim lighting. Scene B transitions to morning with no mention of lighting conditions.

evidence_spans.span_text: He arrived after dark, ate what was left on the tray, and did not light the second lamp.
He was easier with her the next morning than the previous days had suggested he would be.

candidate_findings.uncertainty_note: The transition may be intentional and could be supported by context outside this two-scene window, where lighting conditions are not critical to continuity.
candidate_findings.review_note: Review the narrative purpose of the dim lighting in Scene A and whether it needs explicit addressing in Scene B.

overall_run_note: Identified a potential descriptive mismatch regarding lighting conditions between scenes. No other continuity or progression issues found.
